zh.lifeSummary: 當Katherine Shelley於大約1505年出生在英國素可米爾格洛福時，她的父親Sir William Shelley26歲，母親Alice Belknap26歲。她去世於1560年11月25日在英國素瑞享年56歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Katherine Shelley于大约1505年出生在英国素可米爾格洛福时，她的父亲Sir William Shelley26岁，母亲Alice Belknap26岁。她去世于1560年11月25日在英国素瑞享年56岁。

nameMeanings.name: Shelley
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from any of the three places called Shelley (Essex, Suffolk, Yorkshire) or from Shelley Plain in Crawley (Sussex). The placenames all derive from Old English scelf ‘rock, ledge, shelf’ + lēah ‘woodland clearing’.
Irish (Laois): shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Sealbhaigh ‘descendant of Sealbhach’, a byname from the adjective sealbhach ‘having possessions, wealthy’.

variants.name: Shallow
meanings: Irish (Kilkenny and Wexford): variant of   Shelley  .
English: nickname from Middle English shalou(e) ‘shallow’, conjectured also to mean ‘thin’; perhaps also the same word in the topographic sense of ‘shallow place in a stretch of water’.

variants.name: Shelly
meanings: English and Irish: variant of   Shelley  .

variants.name: Sheley
meanings: Irish and English: variant of   Shelley  .

nameMeanings.name: Katherine
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Pet form of   Christopher ;  also of   Katherine   and its variants. In the medieval period it was a pet form of   Christian   and   Christiana .

variants.name: Christopher
meanings: From the Greek name Khristophoros, from Khristos ‘Christ’ + pherein ‘to bear’. This was popular among early Christians, conscious of the fact that they were metaphorically bearing Christ in their hearts. A later, over-literal interpretation of the name gave rise to the legend of a saint who actually bore the Christ-child over a stream; he is regarded as the patron of travellers. In England the name was uncommon in the Middle Ages, but became very popular in the 16th century, especially in parts of the North.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

en.lifeSummary: When Katherine Shelley was born about 1505, in Michelgrove, Sussex, England, her father, Sir William Shelley, was 26 and her mother, Alice Belknap, was 26. She died on 25 November 1560, in Surrey, England, at the age of 56.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Katherine Shelley est née environ 1505, à Michelgrove, Sussex, Angleterre, son père, Sir William Shelley, avait 26 ans et sa mère, Alice Belknap, avait 26 ans. Elle est décédée le 25 novembre 1560, à Surrey, Angleterre, à l’âge de 56 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Katherine Shelley um 1505 in Michelgrove, Sussex, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir William Shelley 26 und ihre Mutter Alice Belknap 26. Sie starb am 25. November 1560 im Alter von 56 Jahren in Surrey, England.
